title: How This Works
description: A quick tour of the graph browser itself.

stops.title: Welcome
stops.narrative:
This is an interactive knowledge graph browser. You're looking at a graph where nodes represent concepts and edges represent relationships between them.

Hover any node to see its description in this sidebar. Click a node to re-center the graph on it. Use the depth buttons (1, 2, 3, All) to control how many hops from the selected node are visible.

stops.title: Configuration
stops.narrative:
Every graph browser instance is configured by a [config.json](node:config) file. It defines the title, source URL, and — most importantly — the node kind definitions.

Kinds determine how nodes look: each kind has a label, color, and shape. The browser reads these at startup and generates Cytoscape styles dynamically. No kind is hardcoded.

stops.title: The Graph Data
stops.narrative:
The actual knowledge graph lives in [graph.json](node:graph-data). It has two arrays: nodes and edges.

Each node has an id, label, kind (matching a kind from config), group, description, and optional links to external resources. Each edge has source, target, label, and description explaining why the relationship exists.

stops.title: Guided Tours
stops.narrative:
Tutorials are optional. If `data/tutorials/index.json` exists, the Guided Tours dropdown appears.

Each tutorial is a JSON file with a sequence of stops. Each stop names a node and a depth, plus narrative text that can include inline links: `[text](node:id)` for graph navigation and `[text](https://url)` for external links.

stops.title: Search Everything
stops.narrative:
The search box (top right) matches against all text in the graph: node labels, descriptions, kinds, edge labels, and edge descriptions.

Results show up to 12 matches. Click a result to navigate to it — nodes re-center the graph, edges show the relationship detail.
